header.start: {player} must choose who goes first in round 1
header.start.choice: You must <0>choose who goes first in round 1</0>
header.mulligan: Players can exchange cards from their hand
header.mulligan.pass: Mulligan: you can exchange cards, or <0>pass</0>
header.mulligan.done: Mulligan: <0>shuffle and draw {cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}}</0>
header.draw: Draw phase: each player draws 2 cards
header.placement: {player} must place 2 cards on the battlefield
header.placement.me: You must place 2 cards on the battlefield
header.reveal: Reveal phase: players reveal the cards placed
header.initiative.me: You can activate cards with Initiative, or <0>pass</0>
header.activation.pass: You have no more cards to activate, you must <0>pass</0>
header.attack.solve: You can activate other cards or <0>solve the attack</0>
header.activation.moved.choice: {player} can attack with {card} or deactivate it
header.activation.moved.choose: You can attack with {card} or <0>deactivate it</0>
rules.score: At the end of Round 9, each player adds up the values of the cards under his control on the battlefield (Creatures and Places). The player with the most points wins.
rules.tie.1: The player with the most allied cards placed on the battlefield wins.
rules.tie.2: If there is still a tie, the player with the fewest allied creatures in their discard pile wins.
rules.tie.3: If the tie still persists, the game is finally declared a draw.
rules.score.player: {player} has <0>{score} points</0>, <0>{cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} on the battlefield</0> and <1>{discard, plural, one{# creature} other{# creatures}} in the discard pile</1>.
rules.score.mine: You have <0>{score} points</0>, <0>{cards, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} on the battlefield</0> and <1>{discard, plural, one{# creature} other{# creatures}} in your discard pile</1>.
rules.faction-token.title: {faction} faction token
rules.faction-token.neutral.title: Neutral faction token
rules.faction-token.purpose:
Faction tokens are placed on battlefield cards.
They are used to indicate who owns the card, and whether it is active or deactivated.
rules.faction-token.neutral.purpose: When two players play the same faction, one of them uses the neutral faction tokens.
rules.faction-token.flipped: <0>This token is turned over</0>: the card on which it is placed is deactivated.
rules.battle-mat.title: The battle mat
rules.battle-mat.purpose: The battle mat represents the playing zone on which the players will compete.
rules.battle-mat.players: Each player has a slot for his deck, for his discard pile and an <0>Astrale Plane</0>.
rules.battle-mat.battlefield: In the center of the battlefield, the 4 green squares represent the starting zone, where the first player deploys his cards in the first round.
rules.astral-plane.title: Astral plane
rules.astral-plane.purpose:
Cards with the <0/> symbol must be placed on the astral
plane. Please note: the astral plane is not part of the
battlefield.
rules.round-track.title: Round track
rules.round-track.purpose: A game of AracKhan Wars consists of 9 rounds. This track shows the current round.
rules.discard.title: {player}’s discard
rules.discard.content: There {number, plural, one{is # card} other{are # cards}} in {player}’s discard.
rules.discard.content.mine: There {number, plural, one{is # card} other{are # cards}} in your discard.
rules.card.deck.mine: This card is in your deck.
rules.card.hand.mine: This card is in your hand.
rules.card.battlefield: This card is on the battlefield under {player}'s control.
rules.card.battlefield.mine: This card is on the battlefield under your control.
rules.card.astral-plane: This card is in {player}'s Astral Plane.
rules.card.astral-plane.mine: This card is in your Astral Plane.
rules.card.creature: It is a <0>Creature</0> with an Attack value of <0>{attack}</0> and a Defense value of <0>{defense}</0>.
rules.card.spell.active: This is a <0>Spell</0>. Once played, it is discarded after activation or at the end of the owner's turn.
rules.card.spell.passive: This is a <0>Spell</0>. Once played, it is discarded at the end of the round.
rules.card.spell.astral: <0>Astral</0>: this spell is played in the player's Astral Plane.
rules.card.land: It's a <0>Land</0> with a defense value of <0>{defense}</0>. In case of a successful attack, a Land is not destroyed, it is captured.
rules.card.family: Family: <0>{family}</0>. Some game mechanics only affect cards of the same family.
rules.attribute.1: This card can be activated during the Initiative sequence.
rules.attribute.2: This card can move {distance, plural, one{# space} other{# spaces}} before attacking.
rules.attribute.3: This card can move anywhere before attacking.
rules.attribute.4: When this card attacks, it performs a simultaneous attack on each adjacent enemy.
rules.attribute.5: This card can attack enemies up to {distance} spaces away.
rules.attribute.6: This card gains +1 attack for every other allied card sharing the same family.
rules.attribute.7: When this card is active and should be destroyed by an enemy attack, it is deactivated instead. Does not work if the card is attacked by a spell.
rules.attribute.8: Increases by 1 the distance separating them from adjacent enemy cards attacking them (except for spells).
rules.attribute.9: If this card makes a successful attack, it attacks the enemy on the next space in the same direction (if any), with an attack strength reduced by 1, and so on as long as the attack succeeds.
rules.card.value: Value: <0>{value}</0> (top right corner). After the End phase of round 9, each player adds up the values of their cards still present on the battlefield that have their faction tokens on them. The player with the highest score wins the game.
rules.card.deck-value: Secondary value: <0>{value}</0> (top right corner). Replaces card value when building a deck.
rules.card.legendary: <0>Legendary</0> (top left corner). This card is limited to 1 copy when building a deck.
rules.card.limit: Limit: <0>{limit}</0> (top left corner). This card is limited to {limit} copies when building a deck.
mimicry.target.choose: Mimicry: choose the target allied creature
mimicry.target.choice: Mimicry: {player} chooses the target allied creature
mimicry.mimic.choose: Mimicry: choose the creature to mimic
mimicry.mimic.choice: Mimicry: {player} chooses the creature to mimic
horse.of.avalon.choose: Horse of Avalon: place a creature
rules.card.effects: This card is impacted by other cards:
tuto.hand: This is your hand of cards with which you can compete against your opponent.
tuto.placement.1:
Each round begins with the placement phase. The first player places 2 cards face down on the battlefield, then the other player does the same.
<0>The first player changes each round.</0>
tuto.start-zone: In the first round, the first player places his cards in the starting area.
tuto.place-card: Take the card <0>{card}</0> in your hand and place it here.
tuto.placement.validation: Once you've placed your cards, click <0>Validate</0> at the top to pass the hand to the other player.
tuto.placement.2: The second player is not restricted to the starting area, but each card placed must be adjacent to another card.
tuto.reveal:
After each player has placed 2 cards face-down, the 4 cards are revealed.
A faction token is placed on each creature. On this face, it indicates that the creature is active.
tuto.activation:
It's now the <0>activation phase</0>!
Starting with the first player, each player can activate each of their cards once, usually to attack the opponent's cards.
tuto.creatures: The 4 cards placed on the battlefield are <0>creatures</0>.
tuto.attack.value: Each creature has a <0>attack value</0>, displayed here.
tuto.defense.value: Each creature also has a <0>defense value</0>, displayed here.
tuto.attack.rule: For an attack to succeed, the attack value must be strictly greater than the defense value of the card being attacked.
tuto.attack: Move your <0>{card1}</0> onto the opponent's <0>{card2}</0> to attack!
tuto.attack.solve:
The attack is planned. The skull indicates that the enemy creature will be destroyed and go into the opponent's discard pile.
Click "<0>solve the attack</0>" at the top.
tuto.deactivate: The token on your <0>{card}</0> is flipped to indicate that the card is deactivated: it can no longer act in this round.
tuto.movement:
Your <0>{card}</0> has an attribute <1>Move 2</1>.
It can move 2 spaces (without crossing enemy cards).
tuto.move.penguin: Move your <0>{card1}</0> above your <0>{card2}</0> to keep it safe from the hostile <0>{card3}</0>!
tuto.pass.1: Congratulations! You have no more cards to activate. Click on <0>Pass</0> at the top to give the hand to your opponent.
tuto.end-round-1: Your opponent has passed. After the activation phase, the cards are reactivated, <0>each player draws 2 cards</0> and a new round begins. This time, your opponent places and activates his cards first!
tuto.skills:
Your <0>{card1}</0> has a skill that improves the defense of adjacent creatures.
It may allow us to protect our <0>{card2}</0>!
tuto.spell: <0>{card}</0> is a spell. A spell cannot be attacked by the opponent, but is discarded after use or at the end of the round at the latest.
tuto.astral: Some spells with the <0/> symbol are not played on the battlefield but in the player's Astral Plane.
tuto.activation.2:
Your <0>{card1}</0> has been destroyed, but your <0>{card2}</0> has survived thanks to the support of your <0>{card3}</0>.
It's time to strike back!
tuto.group-attack:
In <0>AracKhan Wars</0>, cards can group their attacks.
Start by attacking the <1>{card1}</1> with your <1>{card2}</1>
tuto.attack.meteor:
It's not enough to kill the <1>{card1}</1>, but your <1>{card2}</1> has the attribute <0>Ranged Attack 3</0>.
This is enough to attack the <1>{card1}</1>. Its ability doesn't protect it from group attacks with a spell!
tuto.pass.2: Bravo! Your <0>{card1}</0> won't be able to kill the <0>{card2}</0> opponent, you can <1>Pass</1>
tuto.land:
In addition to <0>Creatures</0> and <0>Spells</0>, there's a 3rd type of card in AracKhan Wars: <0>Lands</0>.
They can't attack, but offer powerful benefits. Be careful, though: if a Land is defeated, it passes under the control of the opposing player!
tuto.help: The cards have many different attributes, skills or effects: click on the cards to find out what they do!
tuto.advice: So making your cards survive is just as important as destroying your opponents' cards.
tuto.end: Now it's your turn to play. Have fun and good luck!
ability.attack.condition.even-value: can only attack enemy cards with an even value.
ability.attacked.limit.creatures: {targets} cannot be attacked by enemy creatures.
ability.attacked.limit.group-creatures: Enemy creatures cannot attack <1>{card}</1> in a group attack. (one creature can group with spells).
ability.attacked.condition.creatures-in-: Enemy creatures can only attack <1>{card}</1> with a group attack.
ability.trigger.fail-attack.destroy: if any of the <1>{card}</1>'s attacks fails, it is destroyed.
ability.deactivate: {targets} are deactivated. They are reactivated when they are no longer affected.
action.1087:
During your turn, replace the characteristics of a target allied creature with the ones of another creature on the battlefield.
If the target creature is deactivated, reactivate it.
action.1134:
Discard <1>Horse of Avalon</1> and replace it with a creature from your hand.
This creature can be activated.
card.name.1110: Hero of the Battle of Nerz
